license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7khhkt6
title: Ligation Protocol with T4 DNA Ligase
author: Alba Balletbó

steps_raw.instruction: Thaw the T4 DNA Ligase Buffer and resuspended at room temperature. 'Pro-tip' : Alicuote the 10x buffer less concentrated so when thawing the DTT gets soluble more easily.

Set up the reaction in a microcentrifuge tube on ice (T4 DNA Ligase should be added last.): %s %s AB1COMPONENT20 μl REACTION2T4 DNA Ligase Buffer (10X)*2 μl3Vector DNA (4 kb)50 ng (0.020 pmol)4Insert DNA (1 kb)37.5 ng (0.060 pmol)5Nuclease-free waterto 20 μl6T4 DNA Ligase 1 μl
Gently mix the reaction by pipetting up and down and microfuge briefly.
For cohesive (sticky) ends, incubate at 16ºC overnight or room temperature for 10 minutes. For blunt ends or single base overhangs, incubate at 16ºC overnight or room temperature for 2 hours.
Chill on ice and transform 1-5 μL of the reaction into 50 μL competent cells (See Protocols for Transformation).
